Description: The difference in implied volatility between at-the-money call and put options.
The difference between implied earnings volatility and realized volatility, indicating potential mispricing.
The five-day moving average of the implied volatility attributed to upcoming earnings events.
The realized volatility of a stock calculated over a five-day period.
The level of implied volatility attributed to upcoming earnings events for a stock.
The ratio of the implied volatility spread between call and put options to the at-the-money volatility level.
The actual price movement of a stock on its most recent earnings announcement date.
The expected price movement of a stock due to earnings risk as forecasted by the model.
The trailing three-year average of actual price movements on earnings announcement dates.
